english: 1. v. sé-ba (se ba). — 2. v. yséd-pa (gsed pa).
Sch. a file (instrument), v. sag-ydar (sag gdar).
small stones; yseg-seb-čan (gseg seb can) full of small stones.

1. cleft, chink, crevice, fissure, leak, v. koṅ-séṅ (kong seng) sub koṅ (kong) extr.; sgo-yséṅ (sgo gseng) chink of a door Tar.; yseṅ bsrúb-pa (gseng bsrub pa) Sch. to stop up, plug up, crevices etc.; yséṅs-pa (gsengs pa) leaky, cracked, full of fissures Sch. — 2. harmonious, well-sounding. — 3. rná-ba yseṅ (rna ba gseng) Sch.: a sharp, acute, quick ear, cf. ysáṅ-ba (gsang ba) II, 2.
also bséd-pa (bsed pa) (pf. yses? (gses?)) to pick, sort, assort, hair, wool; to pull or pluck in pieces.

I. stallion, a male horse or camel. II. also seb (seb), 1. the narrow interstices between persons or things thronged together, hence with na (na), tu (tu) and la (la), between, among, with nas (nas) from between, kyi mii yséb-la mčoṅ (kyi mi'i gseb la mchong) the dog leaps into the midst of the people, ló-mai yséb-tu ǰóg-pa (lo ma'i gseb tu 'jog pa) to put between leaves, sprin-gyi yséb-nas lus p̊yed t̊on (sprin gyi gseb nas lus phyed thon) half of his body protrudes from between the clouds, = rises above etc.; groṅ-yséb-tu bžúgs-pa (grong gseb tu bzhugs pa) to sit among the villagers; k̊yéd-čag-gi yséb-na Meu-dgál byá-bai bud-méd yód-dam? (khyed cag gi gseb na me'u dgal bya ba'i bud med yod dam?) is there a woman among you named Meudgal? yséb-lam (gseb lam) a secret path, by-way, between rocks or underwood. — 2. multitude, crowd, dmág-yseb (dmag gseb) army, nágs-yseb (nags gseb) forest.

(Pers. زر) gold; ysér-gyi (gser gyi) of gold, golden, ysér-gyi mé-tog (gser gyi me tog) n. of a medicinal herb. Comp. yser-skúd (gser skud) S.g. n. of an officinal herb. — yser-skyéms (gser skyems) v. skyems (skyems). — ysér-k̊a (gser kha) a gold mine. — yser-mk̊ár (gser mkhar) an imperial castle Wts. — yser-glíṅ (gser gling) Malacca Cs. — yser-°gyur-rtsí (gser 'gyur rtsi) prob. much the same as: 'philosopher's stone' Pth. — yser-t̊úb (gser thub) n. of the second Buddha, Kanakamuni. — yser-mdóg (gser mdog) gold-colour, ysér-mdog-čan (gser mdog can) n. of a monastery. — ysér-pa (gser pa) gold-searcher, gold-washer Gram.; yser-p̊ud (gser phud) n. of a medicinal herb, an emetic Med. — yser-°préṅ (gser phreng) a gold chain. — yser-byé (gser bye) gold-sand. — yser-mé (gser me) = ysér-gyi-mé-tog (gser gyi me tog) Med. — yser-ytsó-ma (gser gtso ma), yser-btsó-ma (gser btso ma) refined gold Glr., Pth. — *ser-záṅ* (ser zang) W. gilt copper. — yser-bzó-pa (gser bzo pa), yser-mgár (gser mgar) Cs. goldsmith. — yser-yíg-pa (gser yig pa) 'bearer of a gold-letter', ambassador, envoy Glr. — yser-šóg (gser shog) leaf-gold, gold-foil, foliated gold Sch. — yser-sraṅ (gser srang) one ounce of gold; a coin = 16 rupees, gold-mohur.

pf. ysos (gsos), (b)sos (bsos) (= °tsó-ba ('tsho ba)) 1. to feed, nourish Dzl.; to bring up, nurse up, rear, train, bu (bu) a child, dúd-gro (dud gro) an animal; also ysó-skyoṅ-ba (gso skyong ba), yso-skyóṅ byéd-pa (gso skyong byed pa). — 2. to cure, nad (nad), rma (rma); to stop, remove, to put an end to, ṅal (ngal) fatigue, i.e. to recruit one's self, to rest; to mend, to repair, k̊yim (khyim) a house Cs.; to restore, rebuild, re-establish, what had been destroyed, to kindle again, stir up again, a fire; žig- (zhig-) or šig-yso-ba (shig gso ba) id.; to refresh, recreate, sems (sems), resp. t̊ugs (thugs), the soul, i.e. to comfort, console. Comp. yso-mk̊án (gso mkhan) restorer Glr. yso-t̊ábs (gso thabs) way of curing, manner of healing Med. — ysó-ba-po (gso ba po) physician Med. — ysó-bya (gso bya) the thing to be cured, the disease Med.; yso-byéd (gso byed) the healing substance, the remedy; the healing person, the physician. — yso-sbyóṅ-ba (gso sbyong ba) v. the following article. — yso-tsúl (gso tshul) = yso-t̊ábs (gso thabs). — yso-ríg (gso rig) pharmacology.

for ṅyés-pa ysó-ba daṅ sdíg-pa sbyoṅ-ba (nyes pa gso ba dang sdig pa sbyong ba) to get quit of sin, by making confession to a priest and thus restoring the former state of virtuousness, to confess; also yso-sbyoṅ len-pa (gso sbyong len pa), yso-sbyoṅ-la ynas-pa (gso sbyong la gnas pa); such confession does not entail any penalties, but only a renewal of obligations, cf. Fouc. Gyatch. II, 16.
1. v. sóg-pa (sog pa) II. — 2. v. sob (sob).
sincere, ysóṅ-por smrá-ba (gsong por smra ba) to speak the truth.
Cs. and Lex.; usually ysáṅ-ba (gsang ba).
